signals.headline: BitVM Developer Update: WOTS-Tree implementation delivers 40% reduction in post-quantum witness overhead
signals.content: The lead developers of the BitVM protocol have confirmed the integration of WOTS-Tree (IACR 2026/374) benchmarks into the latest off-chain circuits, delivering a 40% reduction in the witness data required for quantum-resistant L2 settlement. By utilizing Merkle-optimized signatures, the update addresses the signature bloat bottleneck that previously threatened Bitcoin's 4 MB block weight limit. This developer response translates the theoretical gains of the April 2026 academic breakthrough into a concrete implementation path for lattice-based structures, protecting legacy secp256k1 outputs against future Shor-algorithm adversaries targeting the 10,000-qubit hardware mark. Linking these 40% witness gains to the 50,298 unconfirmed commitments recorded at burn-block 945821 provides a measurable path for maintaining L1 settlement efficiency and preventing ECDSA key recovery threats during the 36-month federal transition window.
